name: loopback-widget
title: Loopback capture widget
author: Dikshant Joshi <https://github.com/joshidikshant>
description: The embeddable feedback widget itself: a dependency-free vanilla-JS IIFE that renders a floating toolbar, lets a user pin feedback on any element, auto-captures failing requests with their response bodies, console trail and AI run metadata, and turns pins green when an agent verifies the fix. Framework-agnostic — it runs in a shadow root and never touches your styles.
docs:
Load it with your project slug and a running Loopback hub (loopback-mcp-server --http):

<script src="/loopback-widget.js" data-project="my-app" data-endpoint="http://127.0.0.1:7077"></script>

In Next.js, render it from the root layout with next/script (strategy="afterInteractive"). The widget owns its own theme and reads none of your CSS variables, so it looks the same on every site.
